and the small clique of which he is the

all progress in the County Board. Iv is
not long since one of them was glad to ride

But nothing would teach them a

allowed actual train fare when travelling to
mectings. The present officials who
live at a distance from the city have ac-
cepted this allowance. When, or by whom,
this rule was pasied I have no. knowledge,
but I will aid your correspondent to recind
€ at the earliest moment that he wishes to
parify the Board,

2. When a chairman gives a ruling
which is considered not in accordance with
the rules of the Association redress should
be sought through the higher Councils not
through the press,
